name: Sauteed Greens for Piadini
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fresh spinach or Swiss chard
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the spinach and cime di rapa thoroughly, discarding the darkest outer leaves of the cabbage. Cut off the stem from the cime di rapa and cut the cabbage into quarters.
2. Bring 3 or 4 quarts of water to a boil. Add 1 tablespoon salt and cook the cime di rapa for 8 to 12 minutes, covering loosely with a lid. Drain and set aside.
3. If using chard, cook it in the same manner but use its own water, cooking without salt for 15 to 20 minutes. Cook the spinach by sautéing it in a covered pan with just the water that clings to the leaves, adding salt to taste until tender. Drain and set aside.
4. Gently squeeze out as much moisture as possible from all the greens. Chop them together, but not too fine.
5. Heat olive oil in a medium sauté pan over medium heat. Add chopped garlic and cook until lightly colored. Add the chopped greens, turning frequently to coat evenly, and season with salt and pepper. Cook for about 10 minutes, adjusting seasoning as needed.
6. Serve hot. Serves 6.
From "More Classic Italian Cooking"
From the San Francisco Chronicle, 8/10/88.
Posted by Stephen Ceideberg; November 10 1992.
